agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer notification windows, vehicle class enforcement, and configurable stop duration. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, delivery management, fleet/driver management, and customer notifications.","bounded_context":"Route Optimization — but this feature crosses into Fleet Management (vehicle class licensing), Delivery Management (delivery lifecycle states), and Customer Notification contexts. The primary bounded context is Route Planning, which consumes events from adjacent contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shift","definition":"A driver's active working period, beginning when they explicitly start and ending when they clock off. A shift is the temporal boundary within which route optimization operates."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area to which drivers and pending deliveries are assigned. Zones are the spatial partitioning unit for route optimization."},{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated by the system to minimize travel time/distance for a driver within their zone during their active shift."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has not yet been attempted — it is awaiting assignment or is assigned but not yet completed or failed. Only pending deliveries feed into route calculation."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The act of recomputing the optimized route in response to a triggering event (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation)."},{"term":"Driver Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual position or stop sequence diverges from the suggested optimized route beyond an acceptable threshold."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"The set of the next three upcoming delivery stops on the driver's current optimized route. Entering this window triggers customer notification."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicles by capability (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight). Each class defines what delivery types it can legally and physically handle."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is authorized to operate, which constrains which deliveries can be assigned to them."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful (customer unavailable, address inaccessible, etc.), removing it from the active route and triggering recalculation."},{"term":"Estimated Stop Duration","definition":"The default time allocated per delivery stop (4 minutes baseline), configurable by the operations team at the zone level."},{"term":"Operations Override","definition":"The ability of the operations team to change configurable parameters (e.g., stop duration) per zone, overriding system defaults."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically calculate and assign an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a workflow trigger tied to a business process. The trigger mechanism and optimization algorithm are policy decisions that could change.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN pending deliveries exist in Zone A, WHEN a driver assigned to Zone A starts their shift, THEN the driver receives an optimized route containing all eligible pending deliveries in Zone A."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's zone during their active shift.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation triggers are operational policy — the business could choose to batch recalculations or defer them.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route in Zone A, WHEN a new pending delivery is added to Zone A, THEN the driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Recalculation on failure is policy; the system could alternatively just skip the stop.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active route with delivery D, WHEN delivery D is marked as failed, THEN the route is recalculated excluding delivery D."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Deviation handling is operational policy. The threshold for what constitutes deviation and the response are configurable decisions.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver has an active optimized route, WHEN the driver deviates from the suggested route, THEN the system recalculates the route from the driver's current position."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the next 3 stops window on the driver's optimized route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification window size (3 stops) is a business policy that could be adjusted. The notification itself is a preference, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer's delivery is stop #4 on the route, WHEN the driver completes stop #1, THEN the customer is notified that their delivery is within the next 3 stops."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capability.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical and potentially legal constraint. A motorcycle cannot carry freight. Violating this could result in safety incidents, regulatory violations, and legal liability. The system is in a corrupt state if this assignment exists.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is licensed for motorcycle class, WHEN the system calculates a route, THEN no freight-class deliveries are included in the route, regardless of zone membership."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 4-minute default and the override mechanism are purely operational parameters adjustable by policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN Zone A has no override configured, WHEN the route is calculated, THEN each stop uses 4 minutes. GIVEN Zone A has a 6-minute override, WHEN the route is calculated, THEN each stop in Zone A uses 6 minutes."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Route optimization only considers pending deliveries — completed, failed, or cancelled deliveries must not appear in a calculated route.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Including non-pending deliveries in a route means the system is producing a route to locations that should not be visited. This is data integrity corruption — the route would be objectively wrong.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN Zone A has 5 pending and 3 completed deliveries, WHEN the route is calculated, THEN only the 5 pending deliveries appear in the route."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone assignment is the fundamental partitioning constraint of this system. Cross-zone assignment without explicit rules would break the entire optimization model and create operational chaos (two drivers heading to the same stop).","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver is assigned to Zone A, WHEN the route is calculated, THEN no deliveries from Zone B, C, etc. are included."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver assigned to Zone A with 10 pending deliveries in the zone","when":"The driver starts their shift","then":"The system calculates and presents an optimized route containing all 10 pending deliveries, respecting vehicle class constraints, using the zone's configured stop duration"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver has an active optimized route in Zone A","when":"A new pending delivery is added to Zone A","then":"The route is recalculated to incorporate the new delivery, and the driver sees an updated route"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver has an active route including delivery D","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery D, and remaining stops are re-optimized from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver has an active optimized route","when":"The driver deviates from the suggested route","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's actual current position"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is currently stop #N (N > 3) on the active route","when":"Route progression causes that delivery to become stop #3 or closer","then":"The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A driver licensed for motorcycle-class vehicles only","when":"The system calculates a route in a zone containing both motorcycle-eligible and freight-class deliveries","then":"Only motorcycle-eligible deliveries are included in the route; freight deliveries are excluded"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"Zone A has an operations-configured stop duration of 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for Zone A","then":"The estimated time calculations use 6 minutes per stop instead of the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer's delivery is already within the next 3 stops window","when":"A route recalculation occurs and the delivery remains in the next 3 stops","then":"The customer is NOT notified again (no duplicate notifications)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A route recalculation occurs","when":"A customer's delivery moves OUT of the next 3 stops window (e.g., from stop 3 to stop 5 due to a new priority delivery)","then":"The system handles this gracefully — the original notification stands but no misleading update is sent"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"No pending deliveries exist in the driver's zone at shift start","expected_behavior":"Driver starts shift with an empty route. System should clearly communicate 'no deliveries available' rather than failing. Route should auto-populate when deliveries arrive via recalculation trigger (BR-2)."},{"scenario":"All pending deliveries in a zone exceed the driver's vehicle class","expected_behavior":"Driver receives an empty route (same as no deliveries). System should surface this to operations so they can reassign or dispatch an appropriate vehicle."},{"scenario":"Rapid successive recalculation triggers (e.g., 5 new deliveries added in 10 seconds)","expected_behavior":"System should debounce or batch recalculations to avoid thrashing. The driver should not see 5 route updates in rapid succession — one consolidated recalculation after a brief settling window."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates to a stop that IS on their route but out of order","expected_behavior":"If the driver arrives at a valid route stop out of sequence, the system should recognize the delivery completion and recalculate from that point, not treat it as a pure deviation."},{"scenario":"Customer notification sent for 'next 3 stops' but then route recalculation pushes delivery beyond 3 stops","expected_behavior":"No retraction notification — but the system should track that the ETA has changed and potentially send an updated ETA notification rather than a confusing retraction."},{"scenario":"Driver's shift ends with undelivered stops remaining on the route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops return to 'pending' pool and become available for the next driver or next shift. Route is deactivated."},{"scenario":"Two drivers in the same zone start shifts simultaneously","expected_behavior":"The system must partition deliveries between drivers, not assign the same delivery to both. This requires coordination/locking at the assignment level."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone but no driver is currently on shift in that zone","expected_behavior":"Delivery remains in the pending pool. No route is calculated. When a driver starts a shift, they pick it up."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes stop duration mid-route","expected_behavior":"Updated stop duration applies to the next recalculation. Currently in-progress ETAs should be recalculated on the next trigger, not retroactively changed mid-transit."},{"scenario":"Driver is at stop #1 of 3 total — all 3 deliveries are in the 'next 3 stops' window at route creation","expected_behavior":"All 3 customers should be notified immediately when the route is created, since they are all within the 3-stop window from the start."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, driver hours, and failed delivery rates. Proper vehicle class enforcement avoids regulatory fines and insurance liability. Accurate ETAs reduce missed deliveries and re-delivery costs.","operational":"Dynamic recalculation increases dispatcher efficiency by automating what was likely a manual re-routing process. However, it introduces complexity in route calculation frequency and system load. The debouncing of recalculation events is an operational concern that needs addressing.","user":"Customers get proactive delivery notifications (next 3 stops) improving satisfaction and availability at the door. Drivers get clear, optimized routes reducing decision fatigue. Operations teams retain control through zone-level overrides.","risk":"Vehicle class enforcement failure is the highest-risk item — regulatory and safety consequences. Route recalculation storms under high delivery volume could degrade system performance. Notification accuracy (promising 'next 3 stops' then delivering much later) could erode customer trust.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Primary users — receive optimized routes, must follow or deviate. UX of route presentation and recalculation frequency directly affects their workday efficiency."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive delivery notifications. Accuracy of 'next 3 stops' window directly affects their planning and satisfaction."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration control (stop duration). Needs visibility into edge cases like empty routes, vehicle class mismatches, and multi-driver zone coordination."},{"group":"Dispatchers","impact":"Reduced manual routing workload but need override capability for exceptions the algorithm cannot handle."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement is a regulatory concern. Must be provably enforced with audit trail."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated for a product description and covers the major happy paths clearly. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified: deviation detection threshold, recalculation debouncing strategy, multi-driver zone partitioning, and the notification lifecycle for edge cases. These are important but addressable through normal refinement rather than fundamental ambiguities.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class enforcement is a hard invariant — clearly stated and unambiguous","Recalculation triggers are well-defined (3 explicit triggers)","Stop duration default and override mechanism is clear","Customer notification trigger (next 3 stops) is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["What constitutes 'driver deviation' — distance threshold? Time threshold? Skipping a stop?","How multi-driver zones partition deliveries — no mention of coordination between concurrent drivers","Notification lifecycle — what happens when recalculation changes the 3-stop window after notification sent","Whether recalculation is synchronous (blocking driver) or asynchronous","What 'optimized' means — shortest distance? Shortest time? Fewest turns? Priority-weighted?"],"assumptions":["Zone assignment for both drivers and deliveries is managed outside this feature and is a pre-existing concept","A driver has exactly one active shift/route at a time","Vehicle class is a property of both the driver's license and the delivery's requirements, and this mapping already exists","The optimization algorithm itself is out of scope for this domain analysis — we focus on the business rules governing its inputs and outputs","Customer notification channel (SMS, push, email) is handled by a separate notification service"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
